input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete, copy-paste-ready setup plan:

---

## .NET MAUI Dev Environment — Windows 11 (Android + Windows)

### 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Check if .NET is already installed
dotnet --list-sdks

# Install latest .NET SDK via winget (MAUI requires .NET 8+)
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK
# Pick the latest stable (e.g., 8.x or 9.x):
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9 --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

### 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find the exact MAUI workload ID
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it (includes android, ios, maccatalyst sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

### 3. Install Visual Studio 2022 with Required Components

This is the most reliable path — VS bundles the Android SDK, emulator, and Windows App SDK.

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Community with the MAUI workload via winget
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --accept-source-agreements --accept-package-agreements

# Then modify the install to add required workload components.
# Find the VS installer path:
$vsInstaller = "${env:ProgramFiles(x86)}\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe"

# Add the MAUI + desktop workloads silently:
& $vsInstaller modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet --norestart
```

> **What these provide:**
> - `NetCrossPlat` → .NET MAUI, Android SDK, NDK, emulator, Java JDK
> - `ManagedDesktop` → WinUI / Windows App SDK support

### 4. Android SDK — Verify & Configure

VS installs the Android SDK automatically, but you can verify/manage it:

```powershell
# Default VS Android SDK path
$androidSdk = "${env:LOCALAPPDATA}\Android\Sdk"

# Check installed platforms and build tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Discover latest available API levels and build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
    Select-String "platforms;android-|build-tools;" |
    Sort-Object

# Install a specific API level + build tools (example: API 35)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-35" `
    "build-tools;35.0.0" `
    "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Set ANDROID_HOME if not already set
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
```

### 5. Android Emulator Setup

```powershell
# Check available system images
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
    Select-String "system-images.*google_apis;x86_64"

# Install a system image (pick one from above)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
    --name "Pixel_API35" `
    --package "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" `
    --device "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (required for emulator acceleration)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName *Hyper* |
    Select-Object FeatureName, State

# Enable if needed (requires reboot)
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

### 6. Windows Target — Enable Developer Mode

```powershell
# Required for deploying unpackaged Windows apps
reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d "1"
```

### 7. Verify the Full Toolchain

```powershell
# .NET SDK + workloads
dotnet --list-sdks
dotnet workload list

# MAUI diagnostics (checks everything)
dotnet new maui -n MauiSmokeTest -o ./MauiSmokeTest
cd MauiSmokeTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Clean up
cd ..
Remove-Item ./MauiSmokeTest -Recurse -Force
```

### 8. Optional — Standalone Setup Without Visual Studio

If you want to avoid full VS and use VS Code instead:

```powershell
# Install Windows App SDK runtime
winget search "Windows App SDK"
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.6

# Install OpenJDK (required for Android builds)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.21

# Install Android command-line tools manually
$cmdLineToolsUrl = "https://developer.android.com/studio#command-tools"
# Download the Windows .zip from that page, extract to:
#   %LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\

# Then install SDK components via sdkmanager (see Step 4)

# Install .NET MAUI extension for VS Code
code --install-extension ms-dotnettools.dotnet-maui
```

---

**Quick reference — key environment variables to set:**

| Variable | Value |
|---|---|
| `ANDROID_HOME` | `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Android\Sdk` |
| `JAVA_HOME` | `%ProgramFiles%\Microsoft\jdk-21.*` (auto-set by VS) |
| `PATH` additions | `%ANDROID_HOME%\platform-tools`, `%ANDROID_HOME%\cmdline-tools\latest\bin` |

> **Tip:** Adjust `net9.0-*` target frameworks if you're using .NET 8 — replace with `net8.0-android` / `net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0`.
